neighborhoods.name: Chitre Centro
neighborhoods.description: Capital of Herrera province, the cultural heart of the Azuero Peninsula with colonial cathedral and festivals
neighborhoods.character: Provincial capital, festival culture, colonial heritage

housing.predominantType: Houses and apartments near the central plaza

neighborhoods.character_notes: The historic and commercial heart of Chitré. Most walkable area with best access to cultural attractions, restaurants, and public services.

neighborhoods.name: Los Santos
neighborhoods.description: Adjacent historic town and provincial capital known for festivals, folklore, and traditional culture
neighborhoods.character_notes: Established residential area offering a quieter lifestyle while maintaining reasonable access to Chitré's center and services.

neighborhoods.name: Pedasi
neighborhoods.description: Fishing village on the southern tip of Azuero with beaches, surfing, and low cost of living
neighborhoods.character_notes: More affordable option for budget-conscious retirees. May require a car or longer transit times to reach central amenities.

neighborhoods.name: Playa Venao / Pedasi Beach Area
neighborhoods.description: Surf and beach area attracting international residents with newer developments
neighborhoods.character: Surf beach, emerging expat, laid-back international

housing.predominantType: Beach houses, condos, and new development properties

neighborhoods.character_notes: Area with established international community and expat-oriented services. English is more commonly spoken here than in other neighborhoods.
